title: Port issue on server causes crashing
body: ### Context Aero/ Sable and Voicemod on a Server causes a crash. The line on the console states the port is in use probably from Aero/Sable hijacking the port causing Voicechat Mod to shut down and crash the server ### Crash Report no crash log is generated see pictures posted above

title: Moving a docking connector with rope pulley or mechanical piston not working properly
body:
### Description

Im not sure if this is a bug or intended but it seems weird so I thought i would report it.

A docking connector that is activated (powed on), can only be pulled by a rope pulley or mechanical piston to the opposite side that the connector is facing when the connector is powered on. 

For example, lets say that the connector is facing down and is activated, thus the extended part is below the docking connector itself. If i try to push the connector down, it doesnt work, but if i instead try to pull it up, it works as expected.

Also related to this, but might be another bug itself, when a docking connector is connected to another docking donnector on a simulated contraption. If the connector that is not part of the contraption is pulled up by a rope pulley, the contraption doesnt move with the pulley, but instead waits for it to stop moving and then "snaps" in place.

It's kind of hard to explain so please tell me if something is unclear.
Thanks!

title: Mechanism parts are destroyed when an analog transmission is used in combination with a gearshift, and the redstone signal to both parts is rapidly changed
body:
### Description

Using an analog transmission with a gearshift may trigger Create's "block destruction" mechanic, which usually happens when a part tries to turn too fast, or in opposing directions.

Video
https://youtu.be/F6W8gj7I-PU

Though it sounds niche, is very easy to trigger when using a tweaked linked controller, and breaks my quadcopter build :(

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/MDVdOIp

title: Create: Big contractions is incompatible
body: Idk if there are more things but if you have create aeronatics and create:big contractions, redstone links won't work between sub level to sublevel or sublevel to minecraft grid

title: 2 Bugs - Waterwheel Duplication and Hitbox Manipulation, and Docking Port Teleportation
body:
### Description

When you assemble a waterwheel from the middle block of the hitbox, it makes the waterwheel unbreakable unless updated in some way. If you assembled on one of the out hitbox blocks, it drops its item and detaches its outer hitbox blocks allowing you to break the water wheel and have an invisible hitbox ring of sorts until once again, it is updated by a block placed on it for example. Also discovered the docking port teleportation effectively acting as an ender pearl stasis but for contraptions.

title: Navigation Table Rotation Resets When De-Assembling
body:
### Description The orientation in which you place the Navigation Table will always be the orientation in which it is placed back, no matter how many times the simulated contraption is assembled or disassembled. This doesn't matter for vertical Navigation Tables, but for horizontal Navigation Tables, this can break things. For example, when measuring rotation (pictured below), a reset will cause the Navigation Table to no longer be orthogonal to the Lodestone's plane of rotation, and it won't be able to measure its angle any more. Logs are from a new instance of Minecraft (via Prism Launcher, praise be), with the latest versions of Create and Create Aeronautics installed. A contraption that this bug breaks: A video of the bug in action: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0bf3a5ce-5296-4b1a-9e62-30a0d6f4c801 ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/1Zz8lra ### Debug Information

title: Mechanical Rollers not working as intended
body:
### Description

Hello! 
As title says, mechanical rollers aren't working as they normally should, they are not breaking and replacing (pave) the blocks below, they are just filling when no block is below. I tried this with other blocks for paving and still the same issue, first i thought was a compatibility bug due to other mods but i made a clean instance with just Create: Aeronautics and nothing else. 

Demonstration:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d4b12693-bc8a-4f21-b015-7cd27bbb4171

title: Lighting from sublevels
body: Hey so I saw that Sublevels aren't able to emit light to the man word and other sublevels due to restrictions. I was wondering... since sable uses veil in it, what if blocks that emit light uses veil to mimic the light that they would emit out of it to give the illusion of emitting light... if that would make sense at all... I never worked with veil. I feel like it could possibly work... maybe... just wanted to share a thought that's all as I am curious if something like that could work.

title: Dynamic Lighting on Entities gets messed up when create aero is installed
body:
### Description Aero seems to mess up any sort of dynamic lighting mod by making it so entities don't light up anymore. I was told that it was some sort of mixin issue. I saw that the lights on a sublevel can light up entities that come close... maybe it is something related to that? ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/fRM3vic ### Debug Information

title: Name plate cuts off when on chunk border

title: Optical Sensor makes Fake Rays when Outside Honey Box of the Original Build
body:
### Description For an airship, I was making a rope pulley that constantly started and stopped so that the optical sensor attached to it could work and stop itself from going into the ground (rope pulley make optical sensors not work when moving). When doing this, I saw that the optical sensor would leave fake red glow rays when stopping, however not at the beginning when it first started. I checked the honey box of the structure and it showed that where the fake rays started was were the honey box ended. The only mods I have checked with the only mods being sable, create, create aeronautics, and create connected (otherwise my ship will completely break). ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/X7HcevI ### Debug Information

title: Crash with Runtime Trims
body:
### Description

When you load into a world with [Runtime Trims](https://modrinth.com/mod/runtimetrims), Create Aeronautics and required dependencies, the game crashes. The game does not crash if Create Aeronautics is removed, and the game also does not crash with just Veil and Runtime Trims despite the log referencing Veil stuff.

Mod list;
Create 6.0.10
Create Aeronatics 1.1.3
Sable 1.1.3
Veil 3.6.2 (same as bundled in Aeronautics)
Configurable 2.2.3
Forgified Fabric API 0.107.0+2.0.26
Runtime Trims 1.0.6

Cannot get into game to run this command

title: First-person hand invisible when RenderScale is installed — AMD + Sodium + bundled Veil conflict
body:
### Description

Installing Create Aeronautics alongside RenderScale and Sodium on an AMD GPU causes the first-person hand to disappear ~2 seconds after joining a world. The hand is visible in F5 third-person view but gone in first person. All items affected. Persists for the rest of the session.

**Root cause**
Aeronautics bundles Veil internally. RenderScale modifies the framebuffer pipeline. On AMD + Sodium setups these two interact to lose the first-person hand render layer during world load. Confirmed through bisect of 114 mods — RenderScale is the sole trigger.

**Reproduction**
Install Aeronautics 1.1.3 + RenderScale + Sodium on AMD GPU
Join any world
Hand visible ~2 seconds then disappears
Leave and rejoin — instantly gone

**Environment**
NeoForge 21.1.228, MC 1.21.1
Aeronautics 1.1.3
RenderScale 1.3.5
Sodium 0.6.13
AMD Custom APU 0405 (Steam Deck, Mesa 25.3.5)

**Notes**
Removing RenderScale completely fixes the issue. The bundled Veil inside Aeronautics appears to be where the conflict occurs. Standalone Veil versions 3.4.1 through 3.6.2 all exhibit the same behavior when RenderScale is present.

### Game Log

No Log

### Debug Information

I don't think /create debuginfo would have anything on rendering

title: Gluing sublevel with spawn point to a more massive sublevel causes crash on respawn
body:
### Context

Steps to reproduce:
create 2 sub levels, one fairly more massive than the other
place a bed on the less massive sublevel
set your spawn on the bed
glue the sublevels together with a slimeball
die

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/hHdcNis

title: Waystone mod crashed your game
body:
### Context

Hello, I wanted to start by saying that I followed the beginning of the development of this mod, and now that it's released, it's become my favorite Minecraft mod. You've done a great job! 
I'm here to talk about an incompatibility with the Waystone mod. As soon as you place a Waystone on any physics-dependent object, your game becomes unresponsive: 
-you can no longer place blocks, 
-as soon as you break a block, it reappears, 
-you can no longer save your Minecraft world, and you can't close the game without forcing a quit.
I hope this can be fixed. 
Sincerely
Pokecraft

### Crash Report

So it's not exactly a crash that automatically closes the game, but the moment the game becomes unplayable and you lose everything you did after the autosave, I consider that a game crash.

title: Create Aeronautics breaks Quark's worldgen
body:
### Description

https://github.com/VazkiiMods/Quark/issues/5525

Steps to reproduce:

Verify that corundum is generated when only Quark is installed.
In Curseforge, create a modpack - Minecraft 1.21.1, NeoForge 21.1.288
Add Quark 4.1-476
Run the modpack, create a new world, check worldgen - corundum appears all over the place.
Install Create aeronautics and retest.
In Curseforge, install Create Aeronautics (create-aeronautics-bundled-1.21.1-1.1.3.jar)
Run the modpack, create a new world, check worldgen - corundum cannot be found, even in glimmering weald biomes.
Expected behavior

When Create Aeronautics is installed, corundum is generated in the same locations as without Create Aeronautics.

title: Fix swivel bearing disassembly rotation bug (#618)
Aeronautics error with waypoints near it
body: Whenever I spawn at a waypoint it makes my world just a void until I rejoin when it is near a physics object. Im wondering if the devs can fix this issue? Thank you for reading this.

title: Fabric Support?
body:
I would love to play your mod with my other mods but they only use fabric I would Love if you could port it, I hope this isnt a big ask
Thank You

title: air currents made by gyroscopic propellor bearing go through blocks on the same sublevel
body:
### Description

title: AeroLevititeService not found when loaded in large modpack (482 mods) on NeoForge 21.1.224
body:
### Context

Running Create Aeronautics bundled 1.1.3 on NeoForge 21.1.224, MC 1.21.1 with ~482 mods(Mayview (with Cobblemon)). Game crashes during mod loading with:
Unable to find `dev.eriksonn.aeronautics.service.AeroLevititeService` implementation
The mod loads fine in a small/isolated environment but fails in a large modpack. Appears to be a ServiceLoader visibility issue caused by NeoForge's strict JPMS module isolation between JarInJar mods at scale.
Attaching latest.log.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/3l3YoON

title: Moving parts appear invisible and OpenGL error spam when assembling a machine with moving parts.

title: Graphical bug with rope connector
body:
### Description I have connected a fixed structure to a gantried structure using two handles and a rope connector. In the initial structural configuration (maximum rope extension), the rope connection renders correctly. However, when the rope retracts, it does not display properly and instead all that is shown are the handles with rope tied to them (but not connected to one another). title: Huge performance issue
body:
I have a laptop with rtx 3070 and 16gb of ram
When I only use the required mods alongside aeronautics I get 47-60 fps despite the fact that I get 160-220 fps with shaders on the latest game version. 
I don't run the mod with iris or anything I run it as it is through modrinth 1.21.1 neoforge

title: Create: Connected causes redstone links to not work
body: Create: Connected is the only thing I changed and my redstone links just dont work annymore

title: Wheels and Grindstones aren't affected by belts
body:
### Description

### Bug:  
When setting a subworld on a platform of moving belts, if the subworld only contacts through wheels or grindstones, it will be completely unaffected as though it is sitting on solid ground. This is regardless of rotation, and the wheels do not appear to spin. 

### Expected behavior: 
Subworlds sitting on belts should be moved by those belts, no matter what contact type. Grindstones _could_ make sense if they're 100% frictionless, though this would feel weird, but wheels are definitely a bug. 

### Examples:
In the first and second video, you can see crafts of each contact type not moving. In a third video github isn't letting me upload, you can see the craft with a solid block as the back, which correctly moves along the belt and has friction when flying forward, as expected.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f864d828-be5d-4949-b0d8-3a851e049f7c

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ca6c41a1-cc45-4db1-ac89-da1f6ff18bcf

### Credit:
SniperTheNoodle on the Create: Aeronautics discord conducted this test and sent the relevant files, I'm creating the bug report

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/j4ApiEW

### Debug Information

[message.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27072311/message.txt)

title: Massive Duplication Bug
body:
### Description

When combined with the mod sophisticated storage any time you assemble or disassemble the simulated build any attached sophisticated storage containers will immediately spawn the entirety of the internals of that container wherever its located. the original contents will still be inside the container as well.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/5A5ji2A

title: Supplementaries Turn table sends you and entities to the Sublevel when on a physics contraption
body:
### Description

When you power a turntable (from supplementaries) on a physics contraption) and you or an entity stands on it, they will be sent to the sublevel.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/aSD2yaN

title: Translation file does not work due to naming issues
body: "The Simplified Chinese translation file is incorrectly named zh_CN.json. Minecraft requires strict lowercase, so it should be renamed to zh_cn.json to work properly in-game."

title: Divided the Swivel Bearing block
body:
### Description

I tried using physics simulation and Swivel Bearing simultaneously to rotate the wheels, and the blocks attached to this block fell off the main structure. Then I used the physics simulator to return the structure to its blocks, and the block broke into two pieces.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/2M7sLeH

title: Linear performance loss
body:
### Description I've found that after a while my fps, while using create aeronautics, slowly drops. I've tested it in a super flat world with structures and mob spawning disabled. I tracked it down to aeronautics since the modpack was running fine before it's addition. The problem starts in what looks like a small tps drop then progresses from 120fps -> 100 fps -> 90 fps -> 80 fps -> 20 fps -> 1 fps. There is a huge tps+fps drop when reloading chunks. I've tried in 2 different instances and on 1 multiplayer world, there is no abnormal usage of resources reported by the task manager as well as rivaturner. Exiting the world and reentering solves temporarily the issue. Using NeoForge 21.1.227 Minecraft version 1.21.1 Mod list: - Create 6.0.10 - Create aeronautics 1.1.3 - Sable 1.1.3 - Sodium 0.6.13 ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/6erAdFP ### Debug Information

title: Velocity Sensor display value ignores "Towards/Away" configuration and block rotation
body:
### Description


### **Description:**
In the current version, the **Velocity Sensor**'s "Towards/Away" configuration only affects the calculation of redstone signal strength. It does not influence the numerical value sent via **Display Link** to display boards or panels. Additionally, the direction detection logic appears to be axis-bound rather than face-bound, making it impossible to flip the reading by rotating the block 180 degrees.

### **Steps to Reproduce:**
1. Place a Velocity Sensor on a moving physical entity (e.g., an aircraft or a vehicle).
2. Connect a **Display Link** to the sensor and output the speed to a Display Board.
3. Open the sensor's Scroll UI and toggle the mode from **Towards** to **Away**.
4. **Actual Result**: The sign of the value on the display remains unchanged (e.g., stays at -10 m/s even when switched to Away).
5. Rotate the sensor block 180 degrees using a wrench. The sign of the value still does not change.

### **Technical Analysis:**
The issue stems from a disconnection between the configuration state and the value provided to the Display Link:

1.  **Display Source Logic Disconnect**:
    `VelocitySensorDisplaySource.java` calls `vbe.getAdjustedVelocity()`. However, in `VelocitySensorBlockEntity.java`, the method `getAdjustedVelocity()` simply returns the `adjustedVelocity` variable. This variable is updated in the `tick()` method based purely on a physical Dot Product calculation and **completely ignores** the `towards` boolean set in the `VelocitySensorScrollValueBehaviour`.

2.  **Narrow Configuration Scope**:
    The `towards` setting is currently only utilized when calculating `signedRedstoneStrength` via `maxSpeed.getValue()`. This results in a situation where only the redstone output is "adjustable," while the display data remains "fixed" to the raw physical vector.

3.  **Orientation Logic**:
    The `currentNormal` is determined using `AbstractDirectionalAxisBlock.getDirectionOfAxis`. This causes the sensor to identify a fixed axis (e.g., the positive Z-axis) regardless of the specific face orientation, which explains why rotating the block 180 degrees does not flip the dot product result.

---
*This issue was translated and prepared with the assistance of AI.*
Since I'm unfamiliar with the conventions for writing issues, Gemini is expressing this issue on my behalf. I hope there are no problems.
lthough I have attached the latest.log, but this bug does not trigger any runtime exceptions, console errors, or crashes.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/1K2PzrW 

### Debug Information

 I am currently unable to access the game to run /create debuginfo. 
However, the issue is consistently reproducible across all recent builds of Simulated-Project. 
The core logic mismatch is located in VelocitySensorBlockEntity.java, 
where adjustedVelocity remains independent of the ScrollValueBehaviour state. 
I will update this field with the full debug info as soon as possible.

title: analog_transmission block malfunctions with cogwheel power when Create Connected is present
body:
### Description The `analog_transmission` block malfunctions when receiving rotational power from `cogwheels` if both this mod and Create Connected are present. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Have both this mod and **Create Connected** installed. 2. Place an `analog_transmission` block in the world. 3. Attempt to power it via a `cogwheel` (e.g., from a Create motor or hand crank). 4. Observe the faulty behavior. **Expected behavior** The `analog_transmission` block should correctly transmit the rotation state and speed from the connected `cogwheel`. It should stop rotating when the power source is removed. **Actual behavior** Two primary faulty behaviors occur, seemingly at random: 1. **No Power Transmission**: The connected `cogwheel` spins, but the `analog_transmission` block does not rotate (fails to receive power). 2. **Ghost Rotation / Stuck**: The `analog_transmission` block rotates on its own indefinitely even after the power source is removed. This "stuck" state can only be cleared by providing a block update (e.g., with a redstone signal). **Versions** - **This Mod Version**: 1.1.3 - **Create Connected Version**: 1.1.13 - **Minecraft Version**: 1.21.1 - **Mod Loader**: NeoForge 21.1.219 **Additional context** - This appears to be a separate conflict from the known redstone signal issue between the two mods. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/UNT9IKW ### Debug Information

title: My swivel bearing comes off when using a schematic
body:
### Description

If you place and then unlock a Swivel Bearing, everything works perfectly, but when you create a schematic of it and then place it, the whole structure falls apart into different sub-levels.
https://youtu.be/skBAyhoFTJM

https://mclo.gs/QlXkFNg

title: Minecraft crashes on load: Create Aeronautics fails to find AeroLevititeService (Ponder integration error)
body:
### Context

I'm launching Minecraft 1.21.1 with NeoForge 21.1.228. The modpack includes:
- Create 6.0.10
- Create Aeronautics 1.1.3 (includes aeronautics, offroad, simulated)
- Sable 1.1.3
- Other Create addons (they don't affect the issue, it reproduces without them)

The game crashes immediately on startup, before the main menu appears. The crash happens during Ponder (Create's guide system) initialization when Aeronautics tries to register its Ponder scenes.

The main error in the log:
java.lang.RuntimeException: Unable to find dev.eriksonn.aeronautics.service.AeroLevititeService implementation

The issue is consistently reproducible. Without the Aeronautics mod, the game launches fine.

Full crash report is linked below.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/0vQerfw

title: [Crash] Game freeze if Qlipoth Awakening trophee is placed on a contraption
body: ### Context When placing a Trophee Block from Qlipoth Awakening on contraption it caused the game to hard-freeze, and the only way is to get out is to force stop the game. I attached the latest.log file, as its the only file that's created since its a hard freeze and forcefully stopped by the user nothing else is generated. I tested this on a server with 4 friends and placing it in render distance causes all 4 clients to freeze and need to be forcefully stopped, I tried multiple times, I attached a log of a single player with only Simulated Dependencies and Qlipoth Awakening and FDLib (Which Qlipoth depends on). ### Crash Report https://mclo.gs/9Zusewc | No crash report file was created

title: Crash when pasting a schematic containing a Navigation Table with a map from another world – mapData is null
body:
### Context

The game crashes with a NullPointerException when a Navigation Table attempts to update its target.
The crash occurs because mapData is null, but MapNavigationTarget.getNearestDecorationPos() does not check for a null map before calling getBanners().

This appears to be caused by pasting a schematic that was created in a different world. The schematic carries over the map item, but the map data does not exist in the new world, resulting in a null reference.

steps to reproduce:
rename the given txt file to a nbt file and paste it into a new world.

[111112.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27057644/111112.txt)

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/pfvVw7u

title: Game crash while being on a ship with ropes?
body:
### Context

I was building in a creative world, flew a bit around in a plane i just built (which had some rope on it) 

And it randomly crashed. Now I cannot enter that world again, as It keeps crashing with the same log.
I've seen multiple other posts with exactly the same issue, but I personally cannot identify why it happens. Sorry :/

### Crash Report

crash: https://mclo.gs/BIhq8sx   latest: https://mclo.gs/ZCslOLJ  debug: https://mclo.gs/j76e6wX

title: Name of Language Files Doesn't Valid
body: When playing the mod in a language which is not English, we can find that there are no translation completely. But, there are translations in the .jar file indeed. Actually the problem is caused by the file name. The translation file is named in a wrond form. Take Chinese as example, the file name is "zh_CN.json", but Minecraft will only read "zh_cn.json", which is the correct form.

title: Sails on swivel bearings
body:
### Description

When attaching sail blocks to a swivel bearing it makes the contraption more "floaty" as sails should, but they don't produce any forces visible on the contraption diagram and it looks a lot like they don't give the same drag as they would with a mechanical bearing.
Things I tried to notice this issue were:
-slightly downwards angled wings that were supposed to have ailerons/elevators didn't seem to produce the lift that they should accounting for the angle.
-A helicopter propeller using the swivel bearing instead of a propeller bearing for some more realistic details

unable to find logs from the correct session, also unsure if relevant to this issue

title: Carry-on placed player on airship teleported them 20 million out and froze game
body:
I was on an airship I made and tried to throw a friend off via carry on mod and another mod that lets you also throw things and pretty sure I clicked the airship and it teleported them to (real contraption location) 20 million out.

Felt like this should be known even if nothing happens.

title: Crashing when splitting sublevels
body:
### Context

this same crash has happened twice, both times I was taking apart part of a ship and when I dropped a piece off, as it's easier to break on the ground, the game crashed, and rolled back to the last save, up to 5 minutes ago as this was a server

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/jAiNgUI

title: Create New Age Wires cause memory to overflow when simulated
body:
### Context

Hey! 
Gonna write this quick and short since I dont have much time.

Cause:
Simulating a wire connecter with a wire connected to another wire connector.

Effect:
Ram maxes out for the instance and the game frezzes, then crashes. That world then becomes unjoinable and you will crash if you try to join it. (I have 16 allocated, it managed to use 18.5 GB for the instance)

How to reproduce:
Place down 2 blocks with wire connecters on top a few blocks appart, connect them with a Create: New Age wire (I used the diamond variant.). Glue the block with the connector and simulate one of them with the assemble lever thingy. 


Note: Idk if the attached the correct log, the crash-reports folder didnt generate a report, it was directly in the home folder of the profile and generated with the name "hs_err_pid36372.log". 

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/o9Yp0jZ

title: Copycat blocks placed via the arrow thing(see attached image) need to be updated to render
body:
### Description

Description
When a copycat block(a far as i can tell only from copycats+) is placed using the arrow it needs to be updated(i don't know if that is the correct term but a block needs to be placed within a 6x6 area, it changes) to appear

Image
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oR52egD9viM
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fjhsB62h248

title: Headless Spring Crash
body: ### Context The game instantly crashes upon interacting (not breaking) with a "headless/partnerless" spring - For myself, the game crashed while using the wrench on it, but I suspect changing its block state with like another item (ex, the spring) might cause a crash too - I managed to get a headless spring with a schematic 3 times, but it realy is random and upon relogin inside the game the schematic won't be working anymore so I hardly can repliacte it Goodluck :D ### Crash Report https://mclo.gs/HUffuEa

title: Duplication Glitch with Modern Industrialization
body:
### Description

I found a compatibility bug with Modern Industrialization (https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/modern-industrialization). Due to this bug, it's possible to duplicate items in all storage units from that mod.

How to reproduce:
Assemble and disassemble the physical contraption.

I will also create a issues for the Modern Industrialization developers.

P.S. – The pipes are intentionally disabled manually (whitelist mode is enabled) to prevent item transfer.

title: Compatability issue with Create: crafts and additions
body:
### Context

When you give a blaze burner with a straw from C:C&A on a physic contraption and then try to view that with a contraption diagram the game crashes : this has also been reported to the C:C&A devs

[crash-2026-04-23_13.15.55-client.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27056757/crash-2026-04-23_13.15.55-client.txt)

title: Contraption Diagram GUI shows solid dark grey box on Intel UHD / Iris Xe graphics
body:
### Description

When opening the Contraption Diagram GUI in Create Aeronautics (by right-clicking the diagram block), the diagram viewport shows only a solid dark grey box. The GUI itself opens and the rest of the interface is visible, but the contraption preview area is completely covered by a grey rectangle and cannot be interacted with.
Reproduction Steps

Place a Contraption Diagram block
Right-click it to open the GUI
The diagram preview area renders as a solid dark grey box

Expected Result
The contraption diagram preview should render and be interactive.
System Information

Device: Dell Latitude 3440
CPU: Intel Core i3 (13th Gen)
GPU: Intel UHD / Iris Xe Graphics (integrated)
Intel Graphics Driver: 31.0.101.5382
OS: Windows (64-bit)

Mod Versions

Minecraft: 1.21.1
NeoForge: 21.1.219
Create: 6.0.9
Create Aeronautics (bundled): 1.0.3
Sable: 1.0.6 (NeoForge)
Sodium: 0.6.13+mc1.21.1
Flywheel backend: flywheel:off

What I Have Tried

Updated Intel graphics driver to 31.0.101.5382
Set Flywheel backend to flywheel:off in config
Disabled ImmediatelyFast
Disabled and re-enabled Sodium
Set all graphics options to Fast

None of these resolved the issue.
Relevant Config
sable-client.toml: sub_level_renderer = "SODIUM_REACHAROUND"
flywheel-client.toml: backend = "flywheel:off"
Notes
Sable 1.0.6 changelog mentions a fix for "items being blurry and red on some Intel systems", suggesting Intel iGPU compatibility is a known area of concern. The sub_level_renderer appears to be hardcoded to SODIUM_REACHAROUND with no alternative option, which may not render correctly on Intel integrated graphics.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/LJFSSer

### Debug Information

title: Portable generator stress is consumed for no apparent reason.
body:
### Description Portable generator stress is consumed for no apparent reason, i've had this bug happen twice(? at least) now, and i'm not 100 sure what causes it, so bear with me. it appears that, on world launch (or on game start? unsure), there's a chance for portable generators that are on simulated contraptions to have their produced stress consumed by around 50%. the proper amount of system stress is still present, but it's consumed for an unknown reason. breaking and replacing the engine fixes this and restores the stress network to its proper amount. i'll attach some screenshots below to demonstrate this. right side of ship, properly functioning (engine broken and replaced) left side of ship, bugged consuming more stress, why?? ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/R93Kxt5 ### Debug Information

title: Plane disappeared because I glued it wrong
body:
### Description

I built a plane with the propeller inside the plane and thought it would be fine to just include the propeller in the glue selection box. I then assembled it and the only simulated block seemed to be the propeller bearing, the rest was an animated contraption I believe. I then destroyed the bearing hoping the plane would go from animated to simulated but it just disappeared. 

Luckily it was a test plane. Thanks for reading and for making the best mod the game has ever seen!

https://mclo.gs/ezGqWTd

title: floating sail
body:
### Description

sometimes when breaking a propeller bearing connected to multiple sails, some of the sails stay floating and connected to the structure that the bearing was on without being actually connected (even diagonally) to anything, and you can build off of them. I could reliably recreate this by placing a propeller bearing horisontally and attaching 9 sails to it in a + shape (dimensions 5x5), assembling the bearing and breaking it while it was spinning.

title: Laser passes through entities that are close
body: As I said in description, when entities (including player) get close to laser pointer (~.5 blocks) the laser passes right through them

title: Perpetual Motion Using An Iron Handle That Is Placed On A Swivel Bearing
body:
### Description ### You can pull a Simulated Contraption you are standing on by pulling on a iron handle that is placed on a different sublevel. To achieve this you simply place an Iron Handle on a Swivel Bearing and R-click while sneaking. ↑ this is the Contraption that was used ↑ ↑ And this is the setup, A Swivel Bearing with a block on top, i put iron handles all around the block but you only need one ↑ If Pulled without the Swivel Bearing being assembled it does not work, you have to assemble it first If the Swivel Bearing is Assembled, the entire Contraption is pulled along. Thereby achieving perpetual motion. This should not be possible as the contraption can't be pulled with you standing on it without the use of the Swivel Bearing. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/G6Q7AZN ### Debug Information

title: Swivel Bearings misplace their sub-contraptions when disassembled facing a different cardinal direction.
body:
### Description Swivel Bearings are an essential part of creating interesting contraptions and, when deactivating them, they often misplace their sub-contraptions (The ones they are responsible for rotating). This bug can cause you to lose blocks and have to rework on the entire contraption. To recreate this bug: 1. Make a simple contraption with a swivel joint 2. Physics assemble the contraption 3. Rotate it so it faces another cardinal direction with the physics staff (North, South, East, West) 4. Disassemble the main contraption first 5. Right click on the Swivel Bearings to disassemble them, afterwards Here's the setup i used to recreate this bug: Here's a video of the problem: [![Watch the video](https://img.youtube.com/vi/X6V-ayFlfiU/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X6V-ayFlfiU) ----------- Possibly related to the North South East West directions when rotating sublevels, but I'm just guessing here. Maybe some nbt or state on the swivel remembers being originally places facing West and when it gets disassembled now facing South it tries to place its own sub-contraption to the West again, causing the issue. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/C4dBmk0 ### Debug Information
Client Info ``` Create: Mod Version: 6.0.10 Mod Git Commit: ac0c444d9828da3453ae8cc65338e8de063286fb Ponder Version: 1.0.82+mc1.21.1 NeoForge Version: 21.1.227 Minecraft Version: 1.21.1 Graphics: Flywheel Version: 1.0.6 Flywheel Backend: flywheel:indirect OpenGL Renderer: AMD Radeon RX 9060 XT (radeonsi, gfx1200, ACO, DRM 3.64, 6.19.12-200.fc43.x86_64) OpenGL Version: 4.6 (Core Profile) Mesa 26.0.4 (git-d9e5e36b19) Graphics Mode: fancy PojavLauncher Detected: false System Information: Operating System: Linux (amd64) version 6.19.12-200.fc43.x86_64 Java Version: 21.0.7, Microsoft JVM Flags: 8 total; -XX:+UnlockExperimentalVMOptions -XX:+UseG1GC -XX:G1NewSizePercent=20 -XX:G1ReservePercent=20 -XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=50 -XX:G1HeapRegionSize=32M -Xms6000m -Xmx8192m Memory: 2975624568 bytes (2837 MiB) / 6308233216 bytes (6016 MiB) up to 8589934592 bytes (8192 MiB) Total Memory: 3352692400 bytes (3197 MiB) / 6308233216 bytes (6016 MiB) CPU: Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-14400F @ -0.00 GHz; 10 cores / 16 threads on 1 socket(s) Graphics cards: none Other Mods: Create Aeronautics: 1.1.3 Create Aeronautics: 1.1.3 Veil: 3.6.2 Create Simulated: 1.1.3 Sable Companion: 1.5.0 Create Offroad: 1.1.3 Sable: 1.1.3 ```

title: Game stuck in Saving World screen when exiting world
body:
### Description

I've only encountered this error in one specific world, and this world happens to be the same one I test the mod in every time the mod gets updated.

For some reason, the latest Aeronautics update (1.1.3) caused me to encounter an issue where exiting from a world normally softlocks the game at the "Saving World" screen. The only way to escape is to manually end the task in Task Manager. Unfortunately it doesn't seem to produce any real errors in the logs when I do this.

Just to be sure the world isn't just laggy, I've left the world at the Saving World screen overnight, and when I woke up it was still stuck at this screen.

If it helps I can zip up the world and a .mrpack in case anyone wants to try and replicate the bug

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/04eO4WK

title: Issues #291 and #547 fixed.
body: There are some fps issues, but it's _mostly_ working.

title: Teleporting into a contraption crashes the game
body:
### Description Teleporting into a contraption using the teleport spell from "Iron's spells 'n spellbooks" teleports the player into the void, then the game wont respond to commands and trying to quit the game will result in being stuck in "saving world". after loading the game again and entering the world it'll go back to before the player used the spell. ### Game Log in file ### Debug Information can only do this before teleporting:

title: Redstone links not working whist being a contraption in multiplayer
body:
### Description so I was in a multiplayer server trying to make a plane with the mod, but when I tried to use the redstone links they would not register. I checked in single player and it works fine Single player not a contraption Singleplayer a contraption Multiplayer not a contraption Multiplayer a contraption ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/LzcZrDS ### Debug Information

title: The game crashes when turning on the laser from the Makanism mod on a physical device.
body: ### Context Hi! I've discovered a critical issue. When using the laser from the Mekanism mod, the game freezes completely. I also discovered that redstone doesn't work with the Create Connected mod. Redstone only works if you disable the redstone_link_wildcard = false parameter in the configs. Here's a photo of my setup and the crash report log. ### Crash Report https://mclo.gs/6LXrExR

title: Navigation table showing wrong direction when lodestone in assembled and in not loaded chunk
body:
### Description

Well, The title is clear enough, I don't know if it's a bug or if it's supposed to work this way, but instead of rotating towards the lodestone, if the lodestone chunk isn't loaded when you place the compass, it shows its ship location that 2M blocks away instead of its world location. 

Also, even after loaded the chunk, we need to click lodestone with compass and put again in navigation table to fix it

title: Incompatibility with Create Crafts and Additions
body: ### Context When a powered blaze burner with a straw, fueled with a lava pipe is placed on a physical contraption, upon using the contraption diagram all moving objects (like shafts, cogs and blaze burner heads) dissapear. When a block is placed or removed near the invisible objects, they reappear, but when doing so for the blaze burners, the game instantly crashes. ### Crash Report https://mclo.gs/jB8QPxA

title: ticking entity when placing create big cannon mount with sable
body:
### Context

placed down big cannon mount, got ticking entity error, world crashed. 

mod list:
`  Create                          6.0.10                  (released 2026-04-21)                      
  Create Aeronautics (bundle)     1.1.3                   (released 2026-04-22)                      
    ├─ aeronautics                1.21.1-1.1.3  requires create [6.0.10,), simulated [1.1.3,)        
    ├─ offroad                    1.21.1-1.1.3  requires create [6.0.10,), simulated [1.1.3,)        
    └─ simulated                  1.21.1-1.1.3  requires create [6.0.10,)     ← crash origin         
  Create Big Cannons              5.11.2        requires create [6.0.7, 6.1.0)                       
  Create Confectionery            1.1.2                                                              
  Create Deco                     2.1.3                                                              
  Create Diesel Generators        1.21.1-1.3.11                                                      
  Create Central Kitchen          2.3.0                                                              
  Create Dragons Plus             1.10.0b                                                            
  Copycats+                       3.0.4+mc.1.21.1-neoforge                                           
  Ritchie's Projectile Lib        2.1.2                                                              
  Sable                           1.21.1-1.1.3                                                       
  Sable Companion                 1.21.1-1.5.0                                                       
  Flywheel                        1.0.6                                                              
  Ponder                          1.0.82+mc1.21.1                 
  Moonlight Lib                   1.21-2.29.33                                                       
  Veil                            3.6.2                                                              
  Kotlin for Forge                5.11.0                                                             
  Farmer's Delight                1.21.1-1.2.11a                                                     
  Supplementaries                 1.21-3.5.34                                                        
  EMI                             1.1.22+1.21.1+neoforge   (+ enchanting 0.1.2, loot 0.7.9, ores 1.2,
   create_schematics 1.1.0)                                                                          
  When Dungeons Arise: Seven Seas 1.0.4                                                              
  Xaero's Minimap FP              24.2.1                                                             
  Fzzy Config                     0.7.6+1.21+neoforge                                                
  conditional_mixin               0.6.4   `

the clanker i employ inside my computer believes that it's ``` CBC's CannonMountBlockEntity implements HasMultipleKineticInterfaces. When the mount ticks →       
  super.tick() → attachKinetics() → RotationPropagator.handleAdded(this), CBC's own
  wrapOperation$zfk000$createbigcannons$handleAdded (visible in the stack at                         
  RotationPropagator.java:537) re-dispatches propagation through the PitchInterface / YawInterface
  BEs. So getPotentialNeighbourLocations ends up being called with one of those as kbe, hits the
  List.of(...) override, and simulated's injected addAll throws.```
but its probably hallucinating

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/UX9NTFL

title: Conflict with Custom Machinery Create causes game crash
body:
### Context

When both Custom Machinery Create and Create Aeronautics (simulated component) are installed together, the game can't startup. The log shows a Mixin Redirect conflict.The game did not actually crash; it just got stuck on the loading screen with no response. Therefore, there is no crash report, but I can provide the log file.

Environment: 
Minecraft 1.21.1, NeoForge 21.1.226, Create 6.0.10
Mod versions:
Custom Machinery Create: 1.2.3
Create Aeronautics (simulated part): 1.1.3

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/P3oznvD

title: Deployer Placement direction change with rotation on sublevels
body:
### Description

When using a deployer to place a block on a sublevel (take a wood log for example) the block will always face the direction the deployer was assembled in (in the world) rather than facing the direction the deployer (on the sublevel) is currently pointing at. Example:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3fecf7df-377d-47da-b568-870d9e547228

The expected behaviour would be that the log would place always facing away from the deployer rather than changing direction based on where the sublevel is currently facing.

title: Offroad Wheel Mount Turning does not recognise Comparator/Repeater/Redstone device Input.

title: Game Crashes when using a contraption diagram
body:
### Context

I'm building a flying island and when I started a little bit of thrust then whent to check the diagram it crashed my game instantly. placing a new one down It will open for a few seconds then crash my game. Not a large contraption (like 50x50x25 maybe) but it is heavy, the bottom 7 or 8 layers are filled with super heavy blocks to counter blance the whole thing. 

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/6pC5AAV

title: Game crashes when physics object breaks fragile blocks.
body:
### Context

Had a few optimization mods installed when it happened this time, but had happened before with just the minimum

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/uGTdrCp

title: chain conveyor on splitted physics object
body:
### Description

when u use chain conveyor on physics object and break it in half there is bug that keep rendering usable chain on both conveyors, and if u break both physics objects and assemble new one, chain will summon at new object, but it cant be used. 

### Game Log

NA

NA

title: Incompatable with ServerCore

title: Two bugs: Steering/momentum delayed, assembled build FPS drop
body:
### Description

Steering/momentum/velocity is delayed on this server I play on with my friends, though not in singleplayer. I'm not sure why exactly, but it could just be a config that needs to be changed or the server is overloaded. Whenever I turn the vehicle I'm operating, it doesn't go in said direction, it only seems to go perfectly straight or perfectly diagonally and doesn't do anything in between, so when I'm going at a 15-degree angle, the vehicle just travels straight on whatever axis it's facing. The vehicles also seem to stop when turning, specifically builds that fly, whenever I go to turn in something like a helicopter or blimp, the entire aircraft stops and then turns instead of keeping its forward momentum.

The FPS drop happens whenever I am too close to an assembled build or am outlining any of the blocks within the build, the FPS drop only started happening after updating to 1.1.3, before that it was fine.

Here's a video link to hopefully demonstrate better what I am talking about: https://file.garden/ZdJVN-zs_REHyLdk/2026-04-22%2015-21-04.mp4

title: velocity readout reading the wrong direction
body: so heres the setup velocity sensor -> display link -> whatever display no matter what i do to any of these elements, flipping, turning, changing settings, orientation of the display. whenever my vehicle goes forward the display port reads it as a negative number (and vice versa) even when i flip the values in the velocity sensor. it always reads out the oposite speed i suspect that this has to do with physics objects having a "Front" and "back" but i have no clue how to flip this

title: Was the vr aspect of aeronautics scrapped?
body:
Hey there, I recently tried out create aeronautics in vr and it seems that some things are non functional in vr. So I was wondering if the vr aspect of the mod shown in this [post ](https://x.com/AeronauticsMod/status/1588389702263984129) was scrapped. If it was indeed scrapped after the move from landlord to sable then I hope you might reconsider adding it in the future.
Thank you for looking at this,
Pheniox

title: "Random block ticks was null" Error when attempting to rotate Create: Aeronautics Swivel Bearing.
body: ### Context MC V1.21.1 Neoforge V21.1.227 Create V6.0.10 Aeronautics V1.1.3 Sable V1.1.3 Neruina installed. preventing total save lock-out Summary: World crashes on movement of Create Aeronautics block "Swivel Bearing". [Crash-Log can be read here](https://mclo.gs/ZlOw3YE) PREVIOUSLY encountered error involving same swivel bearing block where rotational force from ANY face of the bearing would fail to engage the swivel bearing. this specific error was at first attributed to incompatabilities with "Create: Connected", However I have found 2 notable descrepancies with this: 1) When trying to replicate this issue on an isolated instance with only create, Aeronautics, sable, and connected... neither of the two above errors persist. 2) removing connected DID NOT resolve the primary entity tick error. Below a screenshot of what i am working with. note: the steering wheel is meant to rotate the bearing which would in turn angle the propellers glued behind it. ### Crash Report https://mclo.gs/ZlOw3YE

title: Contraption glitching when a borehead bearing is placed on a sublevel created by swivel bearing
body:

title: Multiplying resources using barrels from the sophisticated storage mod.

If you take an improved barrel and throw resources in there, and then make it physical, then the resources that were inside will fall out, but the resources inside will also remain. (I've checked on a few barrels from this mod).

title: Nether Portals + Creative Physics Staff Interaction (low importance/potential bug)
body:
### Description

Mod version 1.0.3 
NeoForge 21.1.227 
Minecraft 1.21.1

### Reproducing :
1. Grab a simulated contraption with Creative Physics Staff
2. Enter a Nether portal (both gm1 or gm0 work the same) 
3. Exit the same portal
4. Creative Physics Staff resumes the "connection" to the contraption. 

### **Expected behavior.** 
Not to resume the connection to said contraption and leave it at the current coordinates of the contraption and in its last state (unlocked or locked) before player holding the staff was transferred to the Nether. Staff should be no longer active.

### **Current behavior.** 
**While in Nether.** 
You can't use your mouse wheel to scroll through the hotbar (as staff is still considered active and expects to manipulate the contraption. It goes back to normal upon switching the hot bar via number buttons. 

**Upon returning (through the same portal)**
Grabs the previous contraption automatically (if not swapped the item), often results in it being flung around violently.

**Exiting back to the Overworld via a different portal.**
Staff is still in active state. 
While in Nether if you scroll your mouse wheel upon exist the distance between you and contraption will change, whenever it happens in the overworld on its own or only when you exist back to overworld is unknown (need assistance to test). 
Multiple animation glitches on the staff. Really easy to get contraption clipping though the ground.

### **Untested.** 

1. What happens on the other side of the portal (overworld) is untested due to me not having a second account or assistance. 
2. Using handles instead of the staff isn't tested yet. 

(Additional notes)
Might be related or similar to https://bugs.mojang.com/browse/MC/issues/MC-4581 
(wireless ender pearl triggering with a fishing rod)

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/BG6xfcv

### Debug Information

title: Steering wheels fail to register clicks while in motion
body:
### Description When moving at high speeds, attempting to use a steering wheel while facing perpendicular to the direction of movement will often fail to register clicks. It seems like the steering wheel's hitbox shifts forwards relative to the speed, and at a certain point is no longer aligned with the wheel block at all, making it unclickable. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/PUdQcK6 ### Debug Information

title: Fan washing is weirdly conditional for when it washes on airships.
body:
### Description I feel like I'm losing my mind. I'm trying to make a bulk washing/smoking/blasting/haunting set up on my airship, and it seems to absolutely refuse to affect dropped item where I placed the machine, but the same exact set up in another location works just fine. Items on belts or depots work just fine with no issue, but they are very throughput limited so I wanted to get dropped items to work. I'll use some images to explain and try to not go mad. It seems to work the same with the other kinds but I have just mainly been testing washing. In this image, I am washing directly into a funnel with sand. there's no white particles and it never changes into clay. _However_, in this _other_ spot it DOES. So I started digging into it, added more fans and started testing different things like distance from the fan. As it turns out it _can_ wash if its far away enough, but then if it goes _another_ block out it CAN'T, and it can wash a block away from the water but ONLY against a flat wall and not a funnel. EXCEPT, in the OTHER spot where it DOES I tried to see if there was a spot where i could maybe catch it where my fans are _supposed_ to be, but _no_. putting funnels in front of THESE fans _stops_ it from working. I just want to make GOLD, I'm losing my _mind_ here. and just to check my sanity, I checked to see how it acts on normal, not simulated blocks, and they work just fine. I don't know what voodoo you need to do to fix this but I would _really_ appreciate it if you did, at least for my sanity. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/6XnC5yr ### Debug Information
Client Info ``` Create: Mod Version: 6.0.10 Mod Git Commit: ac0c444d9828da3453ae8cc65338e8de063286fb Ponder Version: 1.0.82+mc1.21.1 NeoForge Version: 21.1.219 Minecraft Version: 1.21.1 Graphics: Flywheel Version: 1.0.6 Flywheel Backend: flywheel:indirect OpenGL Renderer: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070/PCIe/SSE2 OpenGL Version: 4.6.0 NVIDIA 580.126.18 Graphics Mode: fancy PojavLauncher Detected: false System Information: Operating System: Linux (amd64) version 6.17.9-76061709-generic Java Version: 21.0.7, Microsoft JVM Flags: 8 total; -Xmx8000M -XX:MetaspaceSize=256M -XX:+UnlockExperimentalVMOptions -XX:+UseG1GC -XX:G1NewSizePercent=20 -XX:G1ReservePercent=20 -XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=50 -XX:G1HeapRegionSize=32M Memory: 2013289504 bytes (1920 MiB) / 5368709120 bytes (5120 MiB) up to 8388608000 bytes (8000 MiB) Total Memory: 3392363352 bytes (3235 MiB) / 5368709120 bytes (5120 MiB) CPU: AMD Ryzen 9 7900X 12-Core Processor @ -0.00 GHz; 12 cores / 24 threads on 1 socket(s) Graphics cards: none Other Mods: Veil: 3.6.2 Mouse Tweaks: 2.26.1 Create Simulated: 1.1.3 Architectury: 13.0.8 AppleSkin: 3.0.9+mc1.21 Distant Horizons: 3.0.1-b Just Enough Items: 19.27.0.340 Sable: 1.1.3 Create Aeronautics: 1.1.3 Create Aeronautics: 1.1.3 Cloth Config v15 API: 15.0.140 Sable Companion: 1.5.0 Create Offroad: 1.1.3 ```

title: Attempt to unload transport crash
body:
### Context

I built a large drilling rig that can automatically process resources. I tried to unload it using the unloading station, but the game crashed after a few seconds. The symptoms seem to occur when activating the manipulator from the "create" mod, which is used at the sorting station.

Also a link to a document with pictures, if that is also useful:
https://mega.nz/file/HLZgSCha#md6GheS2NAkJZl9mvjWQe_-9s2YAyBkBUo_hF3fcnpI

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/fim30NB

title: Ghost block bug on physics objects

title: When in a balloon, you can boost yourself by pulling on a handle on a swivel bearing
body:

title: Physics begin to breakdown at very large distances from world spawn
body:
### Description

I **originally** made this as I thought there was an issue with forward propulsion on servers but, as I tested more, the real issue presented itself.

For context, on my server I teleported the 3 players (myself and 2 others) about 1 million blocks away since I wanted to be far away from our testing/playground (didn't feel like regenerating the world entirely).

When playing around with physics at this distance from spawn, specifically relating to propulsion. they begin to break down entirely.

Not sure how priority of a bug this is since playing at 1 million blocks away from world spawn probably isn't the most common use case lol.

I have no idea what the threshold is, I just know that we experience major issues at 1 million and beyond. (This is the case for any world, not just servers)

title: Rope Improvement Suggestions
body:
1. Ropes should have variable lengths like the springs 
2. Rope connectors should be able to be connected by chains for industrial builds
3. The ropes fired by the plunger launcher should be able to be ridden with a wrench

title: Issue with new contraptions on server
body:
### Description

I have an issue about block updates on a server (10 gb of ram) where new contramprions when assembled have a huge lag on block updates like levers on typewrites. For example if I use a throttole lever with a hot air burner when the build is not assembled when I use the lever the update to it's status is instantly on the other hand when it's assembled it takes a good 5-10 second to update it's status. This is a common issue between players and only happens with new contraptions on the server the older ones work as intended. We tried disassembling all older contraptions to see if it changed something for the new ones. It didn't. Thanks in advance

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/XITPufy

title: [Bug] Localization file failed to load correctly
body:
### Description

ModVersion: 1.1.0

All localization files have been renamed to follow the format "xx_XX.json". For example, the Chinese locale file originally named "zh_cn.json" has been changed to "zh_CN.json". 

At least on my Linux device, this causes the localization to fail to load.

After reverting the localization file names back to all lowercase, the problem was fixed.

title: Tire collision or wheel mount suspension issue when in a nested sub-level via swivel bearing
body:
In an attempt to make a car without wireless redstone, I opted for using swivel bearings to turn wheel mounts while also passing engine power to the wheels themselves, however this causes the suspension to feel way too soft, and the tire starts to clip into the ground at heavier vehicle weights. The suspension strength setting seems to do nothing. When I disassemble the swivel bearings, the suspensions and tires work fine again (without steering, of course).

To replicate:
- have rocky terrain
- make heavy car with swivel bearings holding wheel mounts from above
- note difference in driving experience between assembled and disassembled swivel bearings

title: Steam engine shafts visual bug and odd splitting logic.
body:
### Description

When breaking shafts on a steam engine, some strange things can happen with the splitting logic.

The splitting does not account for the gap between the shaft and the steam engine.

When a shaft connected to a steam engine does get split, parts of the shaft become invisible as if it were still connected to a steam engine.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/009b15b7-aab4-4275-b4a8-45ba847d7e8b

title: Levitite support when dealing with multiple sublevels
body:
### Description From my testing, levitite doesn't generate the expected support to it's sublevel when that sublevel has a weight force from another sublevel acting upon it. This is a sublevel supported by a levitite block with 9.0kpg total mass It should be able to support an additional two wooden plank blocks worth of mass, and will if those blocks are added directly, but it will also sink if a wood block on a different sublevel is placed on top: The problem of undetected mass also causes problems with swivel bearings and their sublevels, which, when assembled, produce weight that levitite on a ship holding those bearings won't try to support. My assumption here is that Levitite only calculates how much resistive force it should produce based on the mass of it's own sublevel, rather than the total forces acting on the sublevel, though that's just my guess. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/NXaGi7B ### Debug Information

title: Swivel bearing issue probable cause
body:
### Description

I have figured out that swivel bearing copies the linked sublevel when you copying it with data(ctrl + middle mouse button default bind) which results in single sublevel getting pulled to 2 or more different points in space or even to it self which results in issues described in #364 and #525. Simplest way to replicate, place a swivel bearing -> place a block onto it -> copy a swivel bearing with a ctrl held -> place it on that assembled block -> if original swivel bearing was on a sublevel it self it would start freaking out, if not you need to assemble new one and it will start right after it.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/eYhGG2B

title: Docking Connector disconnecting
body:
### Description

Docking connectors disconnect when player loads its chunk by moving into it, but not by teleporting.
The connectors still think they are connected and will snap back into place on world load or when teleported. The behavior is inconsistent and will sometimes fix itself when attempting to recreate it.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/86010f88-9b1d-4b83-8465-95e64d9e1fdd

title: Powering swivel bearing on a sublevel containg floating blocks causes whole sublevel to teleport 1000s of blocks away
body:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7f148496-c9af-4e49-a1bb-fcaae9ec2650 video demonstrating the glitch
If happen to be on the sub level player on the client side atleast will fall through the world for a couple seconds before being placed back on the ground.

I only know that the sublevels get teleported 1000s of blocks away given the high region numbers in the sublevels folder.  Going from typical world spawn coords to region -42.19.  And upon repeating the bug at that region location the new sublevel ended up in region 3294.3351.

title: Observers can't detect Altitude Sensors
body: Redstone signals from altitude sensors can't be picked up by observers.

title: Potentially Malicious Packet Issues (Create: Simulated)
body:
many packets coming from the client can do things that are out of scope

### **high priority:**

physics staff - can lock/drag without having a physics staff - suggested fix: only allow interaction when player is holding a staff

### **medium priority:**

physics assembler - can assemble/disassemble from any range - suggested fix: limit player distance from block
nameplate - can rename ship at any range - suggested fix: limit player distance from block
steering wheel - can interact at any range - suggested fix: limit player distance from block
spring - can place at any range - suggested fix: limit player distance from "childPos" position
merging glue (slime) - can place at any range - suggested fix: limit player distance from "childPos" position
handle - can pull yourself at any range - suggested fix: limit player distance from block
honey glue - can place at any range - suggested fix: limit player distance from "to" position
honey glue - can change bounds at any range with knowledge of it's uuid - suggested fix: limit player distance from glue (very large range, but limited)
rope - can break at any range - suggested fix: limit player distance from rope (from average position of rope ends with radius ROPE_LENGTH/2 + PADDING)

### **low priority:**

you can request a diagram of a ship from anywhere in the world if you have it's uuid

title: [Feature Request] Wheel Mount Rotation
body: Wheel mounts can't be rotated to face up/to the side. You can achieve something similar if you rotate it with a swivel bearing, but it's wonky

title: Crash when dropping vault from a great height onto ice
body: ### Context Using this setup, I cut the ropes using shears. When the vault hit the ice, the game crashed. ### Crash Report https://mclo.gs/CebU9kg

title: Propeller bearing doesn't generate propulsion force

title: Deployers can cause holes in simulated structures
body:
### Description

Giving a deployer a block on a simulated object allows the user to place blocks within the simulated space. However, those simulated blocks should technically create a new simulated block but stays within the parents space, creating a gap. Any blocks placed off the deployers placed block is still within the parent space even when either/both the block or the deployer are destroyed, allowing for a gap/hole to be made within the simulated object

title: Change format for extra kinetics information to consider languages with other word orders
body: This mod adds the "extra kinetics information" tooltips for goggle wearers, in which the format goes like this: Yet, but the translation strings only give to translate "Information for" through `"simulated.extra_kinetics.information"` but this behavior is undesirable for certain languages with different word orders. In Korean, for example, it would be better to translate it as "XXXX에 대한 정보", having the component name come before unlike English. However, this is impossible with the current language strings structure. Thereby, I propose using "%s" to allow better flexibility in word order instead of having a fixed format. PS) These changes have all been made through the GitHub Web Client, so I haven't tested the code yet. I do want to apologize for this reckless behavior.

title: Clientside Renderer Crash
body:
### Context

Should preface this crash occurred to me when on a server, however given it is seemingly only present clientside and the logs point to it being a renderer problem, I assume nothing serverside is affecting it.

Not exactly sure what is causing it, but the issue became noticeably more pronounced when using larger physics contraptions so I will assume its an issue with general rendering. I've gone through the mods Im using for optimization like sodium, but the issue persists without any of them as the same crash report occurrs with the vanilla renderer.
 
The Crash Report is the Debug log, so hopefully it comes with more detail than the regular log.

body:
### Context

Another bug happened, (dont have logs for that one sorry) and to resolve it i reset player data so that we could load again not near the ship, and when we loaded it again it crashed with an error involving the rope

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/ij9vVEl

title: Server crash upon assembling/powering a gyroscopic or regular propeller bearing when Lithium is on a dedicated server
body:
### Context

The crash is somewhat reproducible, when i was making a helicopter everything was fine with the propeller, then after i destroyed it and replaced it, assembling it crashed the server once, then twice, then thrice until it corrupted it and forced a backup. After the world backup, the original propeller was working fine, but once i create a second one in a different spot, or destroy the original one and place a second one in its place then try to assemble it, it crashes the server again. All of it reproduced with both propeller bearing types. Once i removed lithium from the modlist (both server and client) it's not crashing anymore.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/CWpT78r

title: possibly an issue with old GPUs causing every single thing that ISNT a physics entity to be invisible except for normal entities like mobs

title: Crash With Create: Power Grid
body:
### Context

Like the title says, the game crashes when you attempt to connect two wire terminals from Create: Power Grid on a simulated contraption. For some reason, there is no crash report and nothing useful in the logs as far as I can tell, but I will add them here just in case I missed something.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/X30BsX7

title: Drag force presenting itself on Levitite
body:
### Description

I made a flat ring of Levitite, 4x4 with the center 2x2 missing, sitting in the air. Looking at a diagram, it accurately shows the Levitation force equal to the Gravity force. However, it is also showing a Drag force in the same direction as the Gravity force. As it is my understanding at least, the combined Drag force + the Gravity force is GREATER THAN the Levitation force. The implication on the diagram is that it should be slowly falling. However this is not the case.

Unless I am mistaken in all of this. I understand at slow speeds that Levitite resists movement (aka, high Drag force) but it does not seem like it should show a greater force in one direction and still cause no movement.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/8mtVzln

title: Game Freezes When Using (Any Kind) Of Propeller
body:
### Context

I am aware there are two related duplicate issues open on this matter #227 and #198 (this one had an attempt to resolve the issue with a null check but it got closed for some reason). However i am bringing this up as its game breaking and not something with any workarounds (I understand the team is going through lots of efforts but its one serious issue).

---

The game freezes when trying to have any kind of propeller in a simulated contraption. The issue isn't restricted to just when constructing simulated contraptions fully, even when placing a propeller on an already simulated contraption the same issue appears.

Curiously enough, no intermediate logs appear between the placement of the propeller in the simulated contraption (either construction time or on an already existing contraption) and the game freeze. **As such logs aren't too useful there (plus there is no crash-reports folder).**

The setup ran the same even with and without many other mods (the two setups involved with just create aeronatics and its dependencies whereas the other with a full on modpack).

### Crash Report

No ``crash-reports`` folder found, plus its frozen (Prism Launcher)

title: Weighted Ejector teleports items to any distance when target is converted to a simulated contraption
body:
### Description ### Issue Title: Weighted Ejector teleports items to any distance when target is converted to a simulated contraption ### Description When a Weighted Ejector is bound to a target block (e.g., a Depot) and that target block is subsequently converted into a simulated physical contraption, the Weighted Ejector can teleport items to the target instantly, ignoring any distance limitations. This bypasses the intended 32-block range limit of the Weighted Ejector, as well as the line-of-sight requirement[reference:0]. Additionally, the items arrive at the target contraption without any of the usual Ejector mechanics (such as the required charging time based on RPM and distance), effectively allowing for instant, long-range item teleportation[reference:1]. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Place a Weighted Ejector and bind it to a target block (e.g., a Depot) using `Shift + Right Click`. 2. Construct a simulated contraption that includes the target Depot block. 3. Assemble the structure into a physical contraption (using the Create: Aeronautics assembly mechanics). 4. Provide power to the Weighted Ejector and input items into it. 5. Observe that the items instantly appear at the target Depot, regardless of the distance between the Ejector and the contraption. ### Expected Behavior The Weighted Ejector should respect its standard mechanics when the target block is part of a simulated contraption. Items should either: - Fail to eject if the target is no longer in a valid state, or - Be ejected normally with the appropriate charging time and range limit (max 32 blocks). ### Actual Behavior Items are instantly teleported to the target block within the simulated contraption, ignoring the 32-block range limit and the line-of-sight requirement. ### Environment - **Mod:** Create: Aeronautics - **Mod Version:** 1.0.3+mc1.21.1[reference:2] - **Minecraft Version:** 1.21.1 - **Mod Loader:** NeoForge - **Core Dependencies:** Create Mod (latest version for 1.21.1), Sable (latest version for 1.21.1) ### Additional Context - **Date of Discovery:** 2026-04-20 - **Relevant Block Names:** - **中文:** 弹射置物台 (Weighted Ejector), 置物台 (Depot) - **English:** Weighted Ejector, Depot - **Logs:** No crash or error logs are generated when this occurs. The bug appears to be related to how the target block's state is handled after being converted into a simulated contraption. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/JtUKFWY ### Debug Information

title: Swivel Bearing precision issue
body:
### Description

When quick succession of high speed opposite spinning received, the spinning goes out of bound. Raising physics steps does not solve the issue. This is most commonly found when combining Torsion Spring with Swivel Bering, always causing wrong resting orientation of affected sub-level.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/0pHBCkz

title: Create shafts turning invisible when Aeronautics is installed
body:
### Description We have an issue that as soon as Create Aeronautics is added to the modpack our shafts (and perhaps something else, this was the first thing we noticed) turn invisible. I made clean client only world and the issue occured there also so not only on server and it's not any of the server-only mods. We have tried removing various mods (Sodium, Entity Culling, More Culling, Distant Horizons), we have tried changing flywheel backend and turning it off. Nothing. The issue is not computer specific as multiple people reported the issue when we added it to the live server. I did test only adding Sable, then the issue doesn't appear. The issue appears when Aeronautics is added. Our modpack source code is available at https://gitlab.com/thesociety/transportcraft-lite if you want to try yourself, otherwise ask me anything you might need and I'll try to provide and help with debugging. ### Game Log Client log: https://mclo.gs/TFQIyxB Just incase log from our server also: https://gist.github.com/GameBurrow/7d87e41eb6f9a588c8ee942e6067b6bd ### Debug Information

title: Water visually bleeds through simulated object while using shaders and LOD rendering mod
body:
### Description

I am using Voxy LOD rendering mod, and the LOD water textures bleed through any simulated object while shader (Eclipse shader) is on (have not tried with other shaders).
[https://imgur.com/a/2Vrjq2F](url)

Works fine without shaders:
[https://imgur.com/a/BBeDuy4](url)

Another problem is that if I go out of rendering distance the simulated objects disappear while other chucks stay visible thanks to Voxy. Until the chunk is loaded again simulated objects stay hidden. Please introduce a way to set the rendering distance of simulated objects, if possible.
[https://imgur.com/a/wNc9Yi7](url)

title: Game crashed as i built a simple baloon (literally not much different than the ones on yt tutorials)
body:
### Context

I built a very simple baloon while figuring out what i had to do to make it float. When i found out i had to put more baloons to it, it started floating, and after a while i decreased the power and then the game crashed and i got soft locked.
Then i tried again and the same thing happened, only thing different was the baloon being like 3 blocks longer and 2 wider and the game crashing after not even leaving the ground.

using neoforge (21.1.217), Create version is the latest (6.0.9), same with Sable (1.0.6) and Aeronautics (1.0.3)

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/kDz9HlB

title: Disconnected From A Server while playing singleplayer? Contraption Diagram Issue
body:
### Description

Built Airship, was locked in place with the creative tool. Placed down a contraption diagram to view it, all seemed well however when I broke the block I got an error saying "connection lost" and I was sent to the server list. I found this odd since I was playing a singleplayer world. When I rejoined I had been teleported hundreds of millions of blocks out with just the skybox visible and no terrain or contraption and then suddenly lost connection again, manually tping myself to world spawn disconnected again however a final rejoin left me at spawn with my airship still there. additionally the latest.log flie is too big to paste below

title: Hanging steam engine shaft bugs out when world is reloaded
body:
### Description On paper, steam engines can hold shafts a block away from themselves without the shaft detaching from the ship. In practice, when the world is reloaded without anything else supporting that shaft, it will decontraptionise into this indestructible ghost shaft. It's avoidable by giving the shaft some supports, but its still annoying and as mentioned, you can't easily get rid of these bugged shafts. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/9xxCbK8 ### Debug Information

title: Teleported far away from ship

title: Animated Parts of blocks invisible on physics objects, Please help!
body: ### Description shafts (including ones on the faces of blocks), cogs (including ones inside blocks), and other moving stuff are invisible on a physics object, i already disabled all culling on sodium, but it didn't work notice that the gearbox placed on the ground has visible shafts, but one in a physics object has no shafts? please help me and thank you! ### Game Log nothing ### Debug Information nothing

title: Springs can't be rendered correctly while using shaders
body: ### Description the spring is transparent when using shaders. Using Iteration RP, the spring is rendered as a semi-transparent stuff and at the wrong coordinates Using Bliss/Complementary/Photon, the spring is rendered as a semi-transparent stuff. Currently, no compatible shader found ### Game Log nothing ### Debug Information nothing

title: Jointed complex Swivel Bearing structure causing weird behavior or vanish
body:
### Description After joining Swivel bearing structures together, restructuring one Bearing will cause (only) the 'link' block to disappear. If structureing the other Bearing, the entire structure will vanish. This structure will also work. The must have is having two bearings connected head to tail on each other. While performing this weird test, slime ball is needed to rejoin detached part back to the bearing. While trying to teleport to block posistion (/tp 20483082.00 130.31 20481033.31), I was thrown into Sable reserved location instead of relocating near local positon (where, removing those 2048 before actual location). No sable sub-levels can be observed, and trying to setblock at local position returns location not loaded (chunk not loaded). The behavior is consistent with and without all other mods installed (voxy, sodium, and all other cursed optimization or lib mods, leaving only create and simulated with it's aero and offroad parts), and is consistent before and after the patch (first launch 1.0.2 and current 1.0.3). Since this is quite cursed, if video is needed please let me know. _Graphic cards in debug info is missing, should be RTX 4090._ ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/USk0tkp ### Debug Information
Client Info ``` Create: Mod Version: 6.0.9 Mod Git Commit: e2de2b9a02406ec33ff6d8851ed6f0b56240b6ec Ponder Version: 1.0.81+mc1.21.1 NeoForge Version: 21.1.227 Minecraft Version: 1.21.1 Graphics: title: CC: Tweaked Events - Typewriter
body:
Typewriters now fire `key` and `key_up` events when their peripheral is attached to any computer

This is done via a new `AttachedComputerHandler` class that is really a wrapper for `AttachedComputerSet` so that the `LinkedTypewriterBlockEntity` class did not load any CC: Tweaked classes

The BE starts with a new `AttachedComputerHandler` instance as a public final field (so the peripheral can access it), then, when `pressKey` or `releaseKey` is called, if ComputerCraft is loaded, `AttachedComputerHandler#queueEvent` is called, which will queue the relevant event for any attached computers

As for the peripheral side, `attach` and `detach` have been overridden and call `AttachedComputerHandler#attach` and `AttachedComputerHandler#detach` respectively

title: Winch Ghost Shafts
body:
