title: Propellers continue spinning when source broken
body:
### Description

Propellers will continue to spin when source of spin is broken.
this was also documented in https://github.com/Creators-of-Aeronautics/Simulated-Project/issues/148#issue-4289593413 for specifically Smart propellers, but this applies to regular ones as well.
An example of the bug has been recorded.
https://medal.tv/games/minecraft/clips/mxRMU4Nw5F5-q0RSz?invite=cr-MSxGTmssMjU4MjUxMzY&v=10

As you can see in this clip, once the analog transmission is broken for being faster than the rest of the system, the propeller continues to spin as if it was there.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/ETrx39A

title: Entity hitboxes disconnect when lead onto a seat.
body:

When an entity is put onto a seat in a physics contraption (sublevel?) with a lead the hitbox is disconnected from the entity, you can still hit the entity in the seat like normal but you still get pushed by the "ghost" hitbox. The hitboxes also seem to fix themselves on restart.

title: Torsion Springs Bugging Out
body:
### Description

Basically, I was working on a VTOL, using swivel bearings with gyro propellers on them.
I was rotating these swivel bearings using torsion springs.
I've been working on this VTOL for hours today, and its never had any problems.
I didn't change ANYTHING, but now for some reason the torsion springs don't work right...
Now when I try to rotate the torsion springs, it will rotate fully then start twitching and rotate fully in the opposite direction.
This happens seemingly randomly about 2/3 of the time.
I have tried relogging and reassembling the contraption.
(sorry about the video quality)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/91c54e54-9340-40e3-a123-39e05f657318

title: Redstone components on vehicles duplicated when physics assembler activated/deactivated.
body:
### Description Working with this vehicle at this location, for reference. As you can see, it's on a hill. Whenever I activate or deactivate the physics assembler, I'll hear the sound of a redstone component breaking, and a redstone component (such as one of the comparators or a piece of redstone dust) will drop as an item. However, those components will also remain on the vehicle. By disassembling and reassembling repeatedly, I can essentially farm these components. It appears to be the same single components breaking whenever I use the assembler -- for the vehicle above, assembling will always duplicate the right-turn comparator, and disassembling will always dupe the left-turn redstone dust. Duplication seems to be based on the terrain as well -- the vehicle duplicated both the comparator and the redstone on uneven terrain, but on flat terrain it only produces the redstone. Attached game log is from a session where I only assembled/disassembled on uneven terrain. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/ylfZcOW ### Debug Information

title: Integrated Dynamics Incompatibility
body:
### Description

Integrated Dynamics cables (and cable components, such as readers and writers) cannot be broken when they're placed on an assembled contraption, nor can their GUIs be accessed via right-clicking. You can also walk straight through the affected blocks like they don't exist.

When assembling a contraption that already has Integrated Dynamics components on it, any variable cards stored in components drop on the ground. Disassembling causes any variable cards to drop again, and then everything goes back to working correctly. This duplicates the variable cards.

Despite this, things still appear to *work?* I tested with a redstone reader/writer pair, and a display panel with an inventory reader. But I obviously can't *change* anything until I disassemble the contraption.

title: Crushed exiting Hyperbox
body:
### Context

I tried to place a Hyperbox from this mod on a contraption platform
https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/hyperbox
Entered ok, but when i tried to leave, game teleported me to 2-million-something coordinates and crushed.

Then I tried a Cyberspace from this mod
https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/cyberspace
Entering teleported me to the same 2-million-something coordinates inside this dimension, I tried to tp myself to 0 10 0 to save the situation, but still crushed.
Reloaded and it was ok, exiting cyberspace dimension not registered a platform as a valid floor, spawned me on the ground below.


Something is really wrong with some pocket dimensions an coordinates of contraptions, I guess?
The exact coordinates are: 20481030.5, something, 20481031.5
Crush log is from exiting a hyperbox.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/slbe3Lr

title: SUGGESTION: Rope Sheaves
body: [NOTE: I know the github says to put the suggestion in the Discord, but there isn't anywhere there for them to go in the Discord so I'm dropping it here.] Ropes are useful right now, but due to how the collisions with them aren't that accurate (especially on simulated contraptions), I suggest adding sheaves (pulleys but Create already has a Rope Pulley so I picked the specific name of the component) to allow ropes to go around corners more easily. When making a rope, you can link it to a sheave as a mid-point, but then have to continue the rope to either a winch or a connector, or to another sheave to continue the rope. Ropes with a sheave part way across their length function as two separate ropes with a shared total length - if one of the rope goes taught it will start to extend while tightening the other, and if one goes loose, it will shorten while loosening the other, continuing until both are equally tight/loose. Additionally, if the sheave is higher up than the winch driving it (accounting for gravity), the rope will apply double the force onto whatever it is pulling, but the winch will run at half speed to what the input RPM. This allows winches to more easily raise and lower payloads that are above themselves, such as to raise or lower a sail (or really any other application of pulleys). **_Potentially_** sheaves could have a shaft through them as opposed to being free spinning for a controlled way to move rope between the two sides without affecting the total length of the, but that would just be an extra side thing if added. Maybe as a brass variant, or just if you use a shaft on a sheave? Beyond being useful for moving heavier loads, having long interconnected ropes strung up around a build would be really cool, and would allow ropes to more easily be utilised in crowded areas, such as to go around corners. Also more rope stuff in general would be cool because I think ropes are underused for such a cool feature. Ok thanks for reading :)

title: Rendering of Aeronautics blocks with interactive/animateable parts broken on assembled sub-levels when Chloride is active
body:
### Description # Context On a world with Create Aeronautics and Sodium with Chloride active, rendering of Aeronautics blocks with interactive/animateable parts on assembled sub-levels seems broken > [!NOTE] > This test was not done in a quarantined enviroment, as such there were other content mods active, but I have pinned it down to Chloride ## Steps-to-reproduce 1. Install Chloride with Aeronautics 2. Place one of the blocks in the picture on a sublevel ( the list is non exaustive, only some I found, more could be affected ) ### Game Log Since there is no functional bugs, only rendering I'm not sure will the log be of any use ### Debug Information

title: Redstone components dupe when assembeled into physics object
body:
### Description

When assembling a psychics object that contains components like repeaters, comparators, redstone links and redstone Inductors the components drop from the block of origin without being broken in the first place.
To recreate the bug create a psychics structure that is honey glued together  and place redstone parts listed above. Then use the psychics assembler and you will see the corresponding components get dropped.

https://mclo.gs/Y76lsok

title: Breaking rope causes null exception on world startup
body: ### Context When using a gantry that is part of a physics object, any rope connected to that gantry will break. This causes varying problems. Ranging from reappearing ropes (depending on gantry position) and continuous forces acting on the objects to a full game crash. When the crash occurs, the world state contains a null reference, causing the game to crash on every subsequent loading attempt. `java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "dev.simulated_team.simulated.content.blocks.rope.strand.server.ServerRopeStrand.getAttachment(dev.simulated_team.simulated.content.blocks.rope.strand.server.RopeAttachmentPoint)" because "this.ownedServerStrand" is null` I was unable to recreate the crash while testing to open this Issue. I was also unable to recover the broken world, the only way for the world load to work was to delete any slvlr file which permanently removes all sublevels. ### Crash Report https://mclo.gs/jGZRkWJ

title: Create:New Age generator coils/wires crash the game when being disassembled
body:
### Context

When dissassembling an airborne contraption with create:new age generator coils and wire connectors on it, the game's memory overloads and crashed the game.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/22gbSX9

title: Trying to steer my airplane crashes the game
body: ### Context (a plane crash!) I am trying to build a biplane. Trying to steer the plane left and right using the rudder crashes the game. For the sake of people searching, here is the important part of the crash: ``` Description: Exception ticking world java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "java.util.Set.add(Object)" because "this.ticksPerPosition" is null at TRANSFORMER/minecraft@1.21.1/net.minecraft.world.ticks.LevelChunkTicks.sable$copy(LevelChunkTicks.java:1030) ``` Full log is uploaded below. ### Crash Report https://mclo.gs/KNOApFB

title: Gimbal sensor not outputting redstone signal
body:
### Description

While I was playing around with platforms, I wanted to use the gimbal sensor for something, and not matter what I tried, the game refused to output a redstone signal when the gimbal was leaning in any direction. The video attached shows superglue, but I did also try with honey glue with no success.

https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/nai2uyf5p1nwodw0v0grt/awesomecreate.mp4?rlkey=7qgsfg4otyd6auqxcgx9a7v12&st=ppgvc7zm&dl=0

title: world breakdown when creating a train
body: ### Context While playing in a single player world, I created a mobile structure and a train on it. Then I caused a train accident and got very distant coordinates. At first, I teleported and ended up in my own structure, but after trying again, I actually teleported and found a copy of my train in the air. When I sat down, I was kicked out of the single player world with a "network protocol error." When I tried to log in again, the game crashed without a crash-report file. The world was broken! [debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26873102/debug.log) [latest.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26873055/latest.log) ### Crash Report no report.

title: [Save corruption] Sleeping on a bed attached to Simulated craft and respawning causes corruption to world save
body: ### Context I was creating a large wheeled vehicle contraption using items from the Aeronautics batched release: The craft is already assembled and functional (moves around under its own power) at that point For testing the behavior of respawning of attaching a bed to the craft, I first placed down the bed on the contraption in creative mode, went into survival mode and clicked the bed to sleep at night, went back to creative mode and drove the craft forward for some distance (more than the distance of a chunk I believe), and changed to survival mode and killed myself to test respawning behavior. The game crashed instantly (First crash report at 1:49am) And attempt to join the game again resulted in not being able to load in and instantly another crash (2nd crash report at 1:55am) An attempt was made to recover this save by removing the Sable and Aeronautics batched mod files temporarily, load in the game (it did load when I did that), go back and adding back the mods and load in again, but it still results in a crash with the same `java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "java.util.Set.add(Object)" because "this.ticksPerPosition" is null` error Is this an known issue? And at this point is there possibility of recovering the game save (mostly the assembled craft, did not make a blueprint for it) And what should be the intended behavior if you place a bed on a wheeled/flying contraption like this? ### Crash Report First crash after respawning: https://mclo.gs/JFuP5Th Second crash for trying to relaunch the game after initial crash: https://mclo.gs/Qs1lCha Third crash when trying the method described above: https://mclo.gs/nPAKVVh

title: Supplementaries cannon aiming bug
body:
### Description

When using a supplementaries cannon placed on a sub-level, the cannon can only be aimed at the sub-level it is on and not else where in the world.
Further, cannonballs fired from supplementaries cannons do not interact with or damage sub-levels at all.

Steps to re-create:
1. Load game with latest versions of both supplementaries and aeronautics
2. Place cannon on physics sub-level
3. Attempt to aim and shoot cannon at somewhere not on the physics sub-level


### Game Log

There is nothing happening so nothing showed in the logs.

### Debug Information

The same as before, nothing is happening so nothing shows.

title: Greenish Glitchy Ghosting Overlay
body:
### Description I have no clue what to call this thing or how to fix it on my end. It might literally just be a "my end" thing since I can't see any other reports of it, but here we go. This happens when a balloon is being filled with hot air. Whenever the balloon is being filled, the screen gets that same strange texture. Other probably related issues I've noticed: My hand and items in it barely render (not shown in screenshots above). Similar patterns appear when I try and view my vehicle as a diagram, but instead of it being a clear-greenish the thing literally just renders a bunch of random blobs of solid gray (not like the other graphical glitch I've noticed where the diagram literally does not render, instead it looks a bit like the overlay that you see. Not pictured, but one time I left the range of the balloon and re-entered it, and there was a ghost artifact of my own screen mirrored upside-down. I have no clue how that could even possibly happen I'm genuinely so confused, as I really don't know how this could be happening. The only suspicion I have is a strange mod called "Veil" which seems to be auto-downloaded and that I cannot find in my mods folder, but I assume it's an integrated dependency. If anyone has any fixes for this on my end, please let me know. PS: to be clear, this has happened with both an integrated graphics card and a proper laptop graphics card (granted one probably from pre-2016 but my point is this is happening on both so I don't think it's specifically the graphics card, though perhaps I'm missing some sort of library on my end that's causing them both to act up? Idk. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/V5bthEU ### Debug Information

title: Entering the contraption phantom world after teleporting
body:
### Description

So after I place Stargate or Tranport rings from SGJourney mod and teleport with either one of them from the static world (so overworld) to the contraption I enter the weird buggy phantom world. After which I pass throu blocks and cannot interract with anything even ingame commands and felt like it might be a bug for your guys since that would mean other teleportation gate-like mods are out of the window with compability? Last I have to use task manager to close the game after I do this bug.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/APdBBEE

### Debug Information

cannot run it

title: Crash when using Physics Assembler on a platform dangling on rope... sometimes?
body:
### Context

I was building a blimp - two hot air balloons and then a platform below it. I wanted the platform to dangle from the balloons above, so I used rope with rope connectors from the balloons a platform hanging below. I selected the whole build with honey glue, placed a physics assembler on the platform underneath, and assembled it. The physics didn't like it and the platform dangling below was ripped upwards and got stuck inside the still solid balloons. I couldn't disassemble it because it was glitched into blocks and at an angle I guess, so I tried pulling it out with the creative wand. That didn't work. I noticed one of the ropes that was attached to the now assembled platform was still floating in the air, as though it wasn't assembled properly, so I flew down and broke the bottom rope connector hoping thats why the physics wasn't liking it. Then the game crashed.

I could not recreate this in a new world with a simpler build and the world it happened on now crashes on open so I am not sure exactly what happened. I think when it assembled the bottom platform something happened with one of the rope connectors, and when I broke it the two connectors desynced. 

I have a lot of mods but wasn't using any in the world at the time, and it looks like the error source has a // todo for exactly this so I think this is a simulated issue?

### Crash Report

https://pastes.dev/hB8aw7RN0l

title: Rope winches refuse to play nice with mechanical pistons
body:
### Description

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/72e7938b-7bf1-4470-a165-45d2614309a3

As seen in above video, a rope winch (and anything connected to it) will Freak Out if moved by a mechanical (sticky) piston. 
The magnet connected to the winch was physics-activated seperately, and the entire contraption is set up on a swivel bearing. Not sure if that contributes to it at all! 

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/Bp8LYXW

title: Expand sensor peripherals with autopilot-relevant readouts
body:
Bundles the readouts needed to build rate-damped attitude-hold and altitude-hold autopilots in ComputerCraft.

**Stacked on #273** (rad-suffixed angle variants). Rebased on top of that branch so the two series merge cleanly in either order; once #273 lands, this PR's diff collapses to just the autopilot additions.

| Block | Method(s) | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Gimbal Sensor | `getAngularRates` / `...Rad` | Body-frame angular velocity (ωx, ωy, ωz). Enables rate-damped attitude control; differentiating `getAngles` saturates near ±90° since it reads gravity projection, not the physics state. |
| Gimbal Sensor | `getGravity` | Body-frame gravity vector. Accelerometer-equivalent; with ω completes a minimal IMU. |
| Altitude Sensor | `getVerticalSpeed` | m/s. Finite-difference of projected world Y per tick. Required for altitude/V-S hold loops. |
| Navigation Table | `hasTarget` | Boolean; true when the held item resolves to a target position. |
| Navigation Table | `getTargetType` | Target's registry key (e.g., `simulated:compass`). Lets Lua dispatch on target semantics. |
| Navigation Table | `getTargetMetadata` | Per-target-type map: `{source: spawn\|lodestone}` for compass, `{placer: <uuid>}` for recovery compass, `{kind: banner\|decoration}` for map, `{biome, state, search_radius}` for natures_compass. Never leaks absolute coordinates. |
| Navigation Table | `getDistanceToTarget` | Meters to held waypoint, sampled at the nav table's 11-tick cadence. |
| Navigation Table | `getClosureRate` | m/s; positive = approaching. Range-rate for approach-speed control. |
| Navigation Table | `getVerticalOffsetToTarget` | target.y - self.y, meters. Returns 0 both when target shares altitude and when the target type forces Y=self.y (map decoration, natures compass, magnet); autopilots can treat both identically as "no climb command". |
| Navigation Table | `getBearing` / `...Rad` | Forward-error to target: 0 = target straight ahead of the nav-table arrow, +90 = right, -90 = left, ±180 = behind. Convenience wrapper over `getRelativeAngle` (which uses the raw `atan2(z, x)` convention where 90° means "ahead"); feed directly into a yaw PID. |
| Navigation Table | `getOrientation` | Sub-level orientation quaternion `{x, y, z, w}` (imaginary first, real last; matches JOML's constructor order and existing CC quaternion libraries). |
| Navigation Table | `getHeading` / `...Rad` | Yaw of ship's +Z axis in world frame using `atan2(x, z)`: 0° = facing world-south, 90° = east, 180° = north, 270° = west. Sign is inverted from Minecraft's player-yaw convention (which has 90° = west); chosen for mathematical consistency with `atan2` and with `getBearing`. |

Follows the deg/rad dual-method convention from #273. All additions are pure extensions to existing peripherals; no new blocks, no NBT changes, no breakage.

title: AE2 molecular assembler compatibility
body: ### Context I was testing AE2 on the contraptions system and I found that having an active and powered system INCLUDING a molecular assembler next to a pattern provider and a 1k crafting storage causes the game to crash when the sublayer system tries to save the contraption on unload. with the following crash: thread '' (10144) panicked at src/lib.rs:498:70: no entry found for key note: run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` environment variable to display a backtrace thread '' (10144) panicked at /rustc/de6d33c033441c5880b863f94d7a3ec8cad141bd/library\core\src\panicking.rs:225:5: panic in a function that cannot unwind stack backtrace: 0: 0x7ff83840566f - 1: 0x7ff838426071 - 2: 0x7ff8384153a2 - 3: 0x7ff8383d58d1 - 4: 0x7ff8383f5daa - 5: 0x7ff8383f60da - 6: 0x7ff8383d59e2 - 7: 0x7ff8383cbacf - 8: 0x7ff8383d669e - 9: 0x7ff83842677f - 10: 0x7ff8384266a1 - 11: 0x7ff838426899 - 12: 0x7ff838179ad6 - 13: 0x24388ea76d0 - thread caused non-unwinding panic. aborting. I had the following setup going, it is inconsistent though, because sometimes I can unload it and sometimes I can't. Strangely when the pattern provider is attached to some other tile entity (like a barrel) this issue isnt there. P.S. Seemingly everything AE2 works perfectly fine on contraptions, including autocrafting, its just the unloading and saving of the contraption that causes the crash ### Crash Report https://mclo.gs/6s8sYua

title: textures are not displayed or stretched a lot.
body:
### Description Hello, my friend was having a problem where his textures were not displaying or were stretched out a lot. title: The game crash with create big cannons
body:
### Context

I placed a Cannon Mount block from Create Big Cannons in my world, and the game immediately crashed. The crash happens every time I try to place or interact with the Cannon Mount. The crash report shows an UnsupportedOperationException in Create Simulated's RotationPropagatorMixin, specifically in the getExtraKineticsBlockPositions method. It seems like the mixin is trying to add elements to an immutable list, which causes the game to crash. I am using Create 6.0.9, Create Big Cannons 5.11.0, and Create Simulated 1.0.2 on Minecraft 1.21.1 with NeoForge 21.1.227. Removing Create Simulated fixes the issue, so this appears to be a compatibility problem between Create Simulated and Create Big Cannons.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/aR4XM3B

title: Aeronautics + Create big canon mount cashes the game
body:
### Context

Issue with Create Big Cannons and Create Aeronautics. RotationPropagatorMixin fails to inject into getPotentialNeighborLocations as it returns with an immutable collection Simulated's extra_kinetics.RotationPropagatorMixin cannot call addAll() on collection after, causing a crash. - Minecraft: 1.21.1
NeoForge: 21.1.226
Create: 6.0.9
Create: Big Cannons: 5.11.2
Create: Simulated: 1.0.2


### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/eqRb1vT

title: Failed to launch with Create: Ratatouille
body:
### Description

With Create, Aeronautics(1.0.3), Sable, Farmers Delight, Create: Ratatouille installed in correct versions, the game fails to load. 

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/8NY4JlK

### Debug Information

debug.log: https://mclo.gs/kYtVAbc
crash: https://mclo.gs/LcczYOx
The game crashes in an early stage, so I can't run the command in game.

title: Incompatibility with Leawind's Third Person
body:
### Context

Leawind's Third Person Version 2.3.0

The client crashes when going on third person when Create : Aeronautics is installed on the same minecraft instance.

Tested with only the following mods : 
Install Architectury + LeawingThirdPerson + Create + Create : Aeronautics + Sable mods
F5 cause the crash 100% of the time
(also, bug report also posted over at Leawind's repo in case it's their problem : https://github.com/Leawind/Third-Person/issues/239 )

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/QuEJE4s

body: ### Description скачал эту схематику из дс сел в него начал ехать а он сразу перевернулся, упал и застрял в блоках. он всё ещё твёрдый но блоки на него не ставятся и не ломаются. также посох физики на него не работает. схема: https://discord.com/channels/937435293294919690/1019670020277670018/threads/1495205519927939180 и главный вопрос как его теперь удалить. *он ехал по ровной поверхности. после перезахода он чутка подвинулся но все ещё он в замле - ### Game Log ---- ### Debug Information - [latest.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26869110/latest.log) [debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26869113/debug.log) [launcher_log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26869114/launcher_log.txt) [debug-5.log.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26869117/debug-5.log.gz) схема: https://discord.com/channels/937435293294919690/1019670020277670018/threads/1495205519927939180 а вот он(пружина) тресётся почему то

title: Plunges render not working
body:
### Description When i fire a plunge FROM a Simulated Contraption TO a block, the plunge doesn't show up (but works as intended, just without the render). However, if i first fire the plunge FROM a block and TO a Simulated Contraption, the render works just fine. I don't know if this is working as intended but i belive not. Also works as intended from block to block. The bug: P.S. Take your time fixing this bug since it's not game breaking. title: Springs give the physics entity a dampening/floaty force
body:
### Description

Recreate this by building a cube made from eight separate physics bodies, one at each corner. Connect each corner to its three neighboring corners using springs, set the spring strength to maximum, then throw the structure into the air. You’ll notice it falls unusually slowly.

This likely happens because the spring damping isn’t being applied along each spring’s local linear axis. Instead, the damping appears to be applied as global damping, which unintentionally resists the cube’s overall motion rather than just the spring extension and compression.

### Game Log

This isnt an issue that produces a log

### Debug Information

it didnt work

title: Game crashes if there is any popular create addon (like create stuff and additions or create numismatics)
body:
### Context

Game launches only if there are no other create addons. Also Distant Horisons crashes the game, but i assume it's because of Iris

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/JcQh6vw

title: Clipboard Copy and Paste Crash
body:
### Context

If you copy and paste the settings on Create: Aeronautics blocks (I used the optical sensor), you can place down the clipboard (intended) but when you do there is black pixels on the block, as if there is text on the clipboard (unintended, as normally when you copy and paste settings there isn't text on the clipboard) if you right-click the clipboard the game crashes.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/RZ0gU35

title: world
body:
Мир из трейлера можно где то будет скачать?
Where can I download the world from the trailer?

title: Game crashes on mod building without nature's compass in v1.0.3 - singleplayer
body:
### Context

As per title.  Booting instance without Nature's Compass crashes the mod loading with game error "Create Simulated (simulated) has failed to load correctly: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/chaosthedude/naturescompass/items/NaturesCompassItem".  Adding Nature's Compass back allows game to launch correctly.

Running 1.21.1 Neoforge through MultiMC, mods enabled are Aeronautics, Sable, and Create.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/TVLKhBI

title: [Bug]: NullPointerException when assembling physics entity with Leaves Be Gone mod
body:
### Description

When I try to use the Physics Assembler in Create Aeronautics, the assembly process fails (nothing happens in-game). After checking the logs, I found a conflict with the mod "Leaves Be Gone". It seems like "Leaves Be Gone" is trying to register tick containers in a Sable sub-level where the random block ticks are null.
logs are like these：Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: Random block ticks was null
at ... LevelChunk.handler$bhm000$leavesbegone$registerTickContainerInLevel

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/zsKui0U

### Debug Information

It seems that the latest update to the stable mod has directly caused it to become incompatible with "Leave Be Gone," manifesting as a crash when entering a save file (which worked before). I apologize that I cannot provide a debuginfo, but I can provide this information.
[crash-2026-04-19_17.30.10-server.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26867104/crash-2026-04-19_17.30.10-server.txt)

title: Insane speeds and strange y coordinates
body:
### Description

Using the creative physics staff to move my contraption. I was stuck within the contraption so when i went to scroll it away i started to accelerate very quickly, attempting to stop I let go of the staff and contraption but by then i had been teleported to -3000, -5000, -4000 (starting at 0, 64, 0). I fell in the void without taking damage for 5 minuets before being teleported to y94 and everyhting was normal again. Contraption nowhere to be found.
Other than this Ive experienced no other issues and am amazed at the work you all have done, great job!

title: Using world edit copy and paste on a created contraption causes a crash.
body:
### Context

I got to overzelous with world edit and started cut and pasting ballons on my contraption while it was "Active" and it crashes the game, not just that but bricks the world entirely! not sure if theres a way to stop this from happening, but the bricking is definatly something to be worried about.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/JHPaB01

title: Wont show previev(Glue, Schematic, Spring, and more) when Quality ist fabulous
body:
### Description

When my Quality is Fabulous it wont show the previw of the glue and more, but if i switch back to Fancy or Fast its there.
I tryed it with and without Sodium and it was always the same outcome.

[https://uploadnow.io/f/KlHFy0T](https://uploadnow.io/f/KlHFy0T)(You need to press the play button on the right to watch it in the Browser)
(I tryed to upload it direkt but it failed.)


### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/3trl0h4

title: Allow Docking Connectors to carry energy
body:
Like how Docking Connectors can carry items and fluids, it would be useful if they could also carry electrical energy for mods that use energy (especially Create addons such as Create Stuff and Additions). Since the Interface blocks don't work between simulations, the only way I can figure out how to transfer energy and make electric vehicles is the battery packages from Create: Repackaged.

Also, the issue templates say to discuss suggestions on the Discord server, but there's no place for that there.

title: [Feature Request] Add keyboard keybind control for Steering Wheel rotation
body:
(For certain reasons, I am unable to send this message via Discord.)
**Description**
Currently, the Steering Wheel can only be rotated by holding the right mouse button and dragging the mouse. This control scheme has two major pain points for players:
1. It is very tiring and inconvenient for long-time use, especially in scenarios that require frequent, rapid direction changes.
2. The camera view is locked during operation, meaning players cannot move their sight or observe the surroundings while steering.
**Suggestion**
Add keyboard keybinding support for Steering Wheel rotation. For example, allow players to bind keys (such as A/D for left/right turn) to control the steering, either as an alternative to the current mouse drag control, or a full replacement. This would make long-distance driving and frequent maneuvering much more comfortable, while also allowing players to look around freely while operating the wheel.

title: Game Crash after removing rope contacts ingame and placing them back
body:
### Context

Game crash:
https://mclo.gs/YDdXnkp (when it crashed, this has connector and continuity but those arent the source of the bug)
https://mclo.gs/qXqkL1y (trying to join)

Crash happened when i was placing a bridge (similar to the one in trailer, where each of them is an entity) and i broke one of the rope holders that were attached to normal game, and to 1 of the entities.
Then it appeared on the one next to it (since there was 2 of them) and when i broke and replaced it again it crashed, and no longer lets me join back.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/YDdXnkp, https://mclo.gs/qXqkL1y

none

title: Using a ReHooked grappling hook on an airship crashes the client
body:
### Context

I fired a grappling hook into the balloon envelope of an active airship and both myself and the other player on board (the only two online) immediately crashed. Subsequent attempts to reconnect to the server resulted in timeout errors, and it still showed "2 online" in the server browser. The server took quite a while to respond to a stop command after that, though that might be unrelated.

### Crash Report

https://mclo.gs/2u9vZql

title: Plunger becomes invisible and unbreakable
body:
### Description I built my first airship while in a voice chat on discord with some friends, the SS Floop. I was happy for my dinky little aircraft. I elected to tether it with a plunger while I made a small barge to sail the river real quick. I sailed it around back to the airship and found the plunger invisible. I could not see, or remove, the plunger. the SS Floop was tethered to the top of a dark-oak tree without a means of untethering it without exiting the world. Removing the blocks the plunger was connected too did not sever the connection. -=-=-=-=- -=-=-=-=- As indicated above, I can untether the airship by exiting and reloading into the world. The airship returns to it's proper resting height and can fly away. but the invisible plunger thing is still a bug I thought I should report. the log and debug info are captured from when I recreated the bug. To reproduce the bug, you should tether an airship with a plunger and then, presumably, go far enough away that the plunger unloads and then return to the airship. Or at least that's what I'm assuming is happening. To release the invisible tether you must exit and then reload the world. ### Game Log https://mclo.gs/wgV1VSd ### Debug Information

title: Respawn point is not reset when contraption bed is destroyed
body:
### Description

If you place a bed on a physics contraption and use it to set your spawn, you will respawn there as intended - however if the bed is broken, the player's spawnpoint will not go back to world spawn, and will instead remain wherever the bed was last. This is especially an issue if (for some silly reason) your bed was destroyed in the void, or a lava lake, and would lead to a softlock.

title: Curious Spring Behavior
body:
### Description

When I heard about springs, I really wanted to test them out, and found that they were at least a fun novelty. However, while testing their compression, something unusual happened - The spring was connected directly to an oak log, while I had created a column of many netherite blocks above. The spring was in its thickest configuration and was the maximum 9m long at the time. When the stack fell over, I removed the netherite block closest to the log, and when the oak log sprung back into place, it never stopped jiggling. More interestingly, this constant motion could be inherited by other nearby springs - even those that cannot physically touch the original, and when they're part of another sub-level.

I've uploaded the video to YouTube for your convenience. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uPDYWVXkdZU

title: Laser Rendering Issue

title: Floating Rope
body:
### Description

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1bcca002-f625-46ce-b096-e81e4ea7e198

Floating rope piece that is unbreakable. Does not disappear when disassembling the airship with the physics disassembler. 

I showed the steps I did to reproduce it twice (but it's inconsistent so it didn't happen in the screen recording i sent)

title: problem with texture
Zero block option in optical sensor UI
body: There is an option to set optical sensor to 0 blocks in the UI, even though it doesn't actually set the block to that value and is instead set back to 1 block

title: Non player entities can't sit in assembled contraption seats
body:
### Description

When a contraption assembles, entities sitting in seats get ejected and cannot sit in seats. players still can sit in seats, but not other mobs.
To recreate:
Attach a seat to an unassembled contraption. put a mob in the seat and assemble it. the mob should pop out and refuse to go back. When reassembled, mobs can go back into the seat.

### Game Log

https://mclo.gs/C1cqojA
